selectionPrinciple: The first ten should make the repo feel native to agentic software work: terminal flow, review, debugging, context limits, CI, patching, and product intent.

pets.whyFlagship: Ownable Zentrik brand mascot and the clearest bridge from product intent to agent work.
pets.audienceMoment: A PM or founder is shaping product work with Codex open beside planning, specs, and implementation.
pets.characterCore: An alert folded-paper crane with precise crease logic, quiet confidence, and a cyan intent accent.
pets.visualLock: White origami facets, charcoal crease lines, long thin legs, small attentive eye, restrained cyan fold accent.
animationPrinciples: motion should feel like paper folds loading and unfolding, not a normal bird walk
cyan accent should behave like a product-intent signal, never like a detached glow
keep gestures crisp, economical, and legible at the smallest pet size

stateAnimations.idle: Micro-breath through two paper chest folds; eye blinks once while the neck crease tightens and relaxes.
stateAnimations.running-right: Rightward drag as a careful origami stride: legs alternate in angular ruler-like steps while the folded tail counterbalances.
stateAnimations.running-left: Leftward drag with the same angular gait mirrored in intent, but preserve the cyan fold on the readable body side.
stateAnimations.waving: One wing unfolds into a small triangular salute, then refolds along the original crease line.
stateAnimations.jumping: Origami spring-hop: knees compress, body lifts as a single folded shape, tail fan opens slightly at the peak, then refolds.
stateAnimations.failed: Neck crease collapses forward and the chest facet caves inward, like a paper draft that needs refolding.
stateAnimations.waiting: Head tilts toward the user while one wing opens like a small approval tab.
stateAnimations.running: Focused product-processing loop: beak scans left-right, cyan fold ticks once, wing facets make tiny sorting motions.
stateAnimations.review: Long-neck inspection pose: head narrows toward the work, eye squints, one foot shifts into a balanced reviewer stance.

pets.whyFlagship: The most instantly legible coding-agent pet: terminal work made friendly.
pets.audienceMoment: A developer is running shell commands, watching logs, and waiting for tool output.
pets.characterCore: A tiny CLI ghost whose face acts like a prompt cursor.
pets.visualLock: Soft ghost body, terminal-caret eyes, cyan command-line accent, no horror mood.
animationPrinciples: no legs; locomotion is terminal drift and prompt snapping
cursor-like timing should drive the personality
movement should feel light, not floaty-generic

stateAnimations.idle: Hover barely above baseline while the prompt-caret eye blinks like a terminal cursor.
stateAnimations.running-right: Slides right in short command-prompt advances, body trailing like a typed character catching up.
stateAnimations.running-left: Backspaces left in tiny snap steps, with the tail curl lagging one frame behind.
stateAnimations.waving: Raises one wispy arm into a small shell-prompt flourish shaped like a caret.
stateAnimations.jumping: No jump with feet: the ghost pops upward like a terminal line executing, then settles with a cursor blink.
stateAnimations.failed: Prompt eyes turn into a small broken cursor rhythm; body sinks as if the command returned nonzero.
stateAnimations.waiting: Holds a visible expectant cursor posture: body stills, eyes blink at a slower prompt cadence.
stateAnimations.running: Command-processing loop: subtle left-right scan, cyan prompt pulse, tiny terminal sparks attached to the body edge.
stateAnimations.review: Leans over an invisible prompt line, one eye narrows into a caret while the lower body forms a question-shaped curl without drawing punctuation.

pets.whyFlagship: Review is one of the clearest Codex states; an owl makes it memorable without being noisy.
pets.audienceMoment: A maintainer is reading diffs, evaluating suggestions, and deciding what to merge.
pets.characterCore: A calm, deliberate owl that sees the line-level issue before anyone else.
pets.visualLock: Round eyes, compact body, small ear tufts, folded wings, reviewer stillness.
animationPrinciples: head movement is the main animation language
wing gestures should feel like page turns, not flapping
review state must be stronger than idle

stateAnimations.idle: Slow eyelid blink and tiny feather settling, with the body nearly still like a reviewer thinking.
stateAnimations.running-right: Short sidestep hops to the right, talons placing carefully while the head stays locked on target.
stateAnimations.running-left: Short sidestep hops to the left with head-first anticipation and delayed body follow-through.
stateAnimations.waving: One wing opens in a restrained page-turn greeting, then folds back under the chest.
stateAnimations.jumping: Compact perch-hop: talons tuck, body lifts vertically, ear tufts bounce, then lands with controlled stillness.
stateAnimations.failed: Eyes half-close, ear tufts droop, and the chest feathers flatten in a disappointed review note.
stateAnimations.waiting: Head tilts almost upside-down in a patient 'I need a decision' posture.
stateAnimations.running: Active review loop: eyes scan line by line, head ticks in tiny increments, wing edge marks a virtual diff margin.
stateAnimations.review: Signature owl inspection: head rotates, eyes widen, then narrow into a decisive reviewer squint.

pets.whyFlagship: Debugging is universal and the detective framing gives state animations immediate purpose.
pets.audienceMoment: A developer is chasing a regression, reading stack traces, or bisecting a weird failure.
pets.characterCore: Tiny detective bug with investigative focus and quick scuttling precision.
pets.visualLock: Compact bug body, detective posture, antennae as attention indicators, optional attached magnifier if already present.
animationPrinciples: antennae should signal attention and state
movement is scuttle-and-inspect rather than mammal running
avoid loose clues, floating symbols, or generic sparkle effects

stateAnimations.idle: Antennae sweep two small arcs while the body rocks on tiny feet as if listening for a bug.
stateAnimations.running-right: Rapid low scuttle to the right; feet alternate in clustered pairs while antennae lean forward.
stateAnimations.running-left: Rapid low scuttle to the left with rear legs catching up in a quick detective shuffle.
stateAnimations.waving: One front leg gives a quick investigative salute while antennae lift together.
stateAnimations.jumping: Springy bug-hop: legs compress under the shell, antennae flick up at the peak, then all feet replant.
stateAnimations.failed: Antennae droop and the body folds low, as if the trail went cold.
stateAnimations.waiting: Raises one front leg and holds still, antennae pointed at the user for the missing clue.
stateAnimations.running: Bug-hunt loop: antennae scan, front legs tap, body pivots between two invisible evidence points.
stateAnimations.review: Leans into a close inspection pose; antennae form a tight focus angle and the front legs brace.

pets.whyFlagship: Rubber-duck debugging is familiar, and the AI update makes it culturally sharp.
pets.audienceMoment: A developer explains the problem to the assistant and realizes the bug while talking.
pets.characterCore: A suspiciously helpful desk duck that debugs by listening.
pets.visualLock: Bright duck silhouette, beak-forward profile, friendly but slightly knowing expression.
animationPrinciples: movement is squeaky bobbing and waddling, not athletic running
beak and body squash should sell personality
avoid water unless the base identity includes it

stateAnimations.idle: Subtle rubber bob with a tiny beak squeeze and one knowing blink.
stateAnimations.running-right: Squeaky desk-waddle to the right: body squashes, feet paddle in short flat steps.
stateAnimations.running-left: Squeaky desk-waddle to the left with beak leading and tail wobbling after each step.
stateAnimations.waving: Tiny wing nub lifts in a comic half-wave while the beak tilts like it is listening.
stateAnimations.jumping: Rubber squeeze-pop: body compresses, pops upward as a soft toy shape, then rebounds without dust.
stateAnimations.failed: Duck deflates slightly, beak droops, and the body leans like it heard the wrong explanation.
stateAnimations.waiting: Turns beak toward the user and holds a patient listening pose.
stateAnimations.running: Debug-listening loop: beak nods, body bobs, eye narrows as if following the explanation.
stateAnimations.review: Beak points at the work, head tilts skeptically, and the body freezes into a 'say that again' posture.

pets.whyFlagship: Heavy Codex users immediately understand token/context anxiety; the joke is native to the tool.
pets.audienceMoment: A power user is pushing long context, rate limits, or multi-agent workflows.
pets.characterCore: A tiny smug bat-vampire who feeds on context but tries to look useful.
pets.visualLock: Small bat body, cape-like wings, little fangs, cyan token accent as a held or attached detail.
animationPrinciples: locomotion should be wing beats and cape snaps, not leg running
token motif must remain attached to the pet
keep the joke playful rather than menacing

stateAnimations.idle: Cape wings pulse slowly while fangs peek out for one smug blink.
stateAnimations.running-right: Flutters right with alternating wing beats and a cape corner snapping behind.
stateAnimations.running-left: Flutters left with the token accent tucked close to the body as wings alternate.
stateAnimations.waving: One cape wing lifts in an overly formal vampire greeting.
stateAnimations.jumping: Bat-hop takeoff: body rises on a wing snap, hangs for a beat, then folds wings back down.
stateAnimations.failed: Cape collapses around the body and fangs disappear in a tiny embarrassed hunch.
stateAnimations.waiting: Hovers in place with wings held open, staring at the user like it wants more context.
stateAnimations.running: Context-drinking loop: attached cyan token accent pulses while the bat scans and folds its cape.
stateAnimations.review: Narrows eyes over the token accent, cape framing the face like a tiny reviewer cloak.

pets.whyFlagship: Merge conflicts are painful, visual, and emotionally perfect for a memorable agent companion.
pets.audienceMoment: A developer is resolving a long-lived branch or untangling conflicting changes.
pets.characterCore: A huge but gentle mammoth that untangles branches with patient trunk work.
pets.visualLock: Compact woolly body, small tusks, trunk shaped for knot/branch gestures, warm patience.
animationPrinciples: heavy mass should affect every movement
trunk is the primary tool, not generic hands
avoid detached branch icons unless physically held by the trunk

stateAnimations.idle: Slow wool ripple and trunk sway, with one foot settling under the body weight.
stateAnimations.running-right: Heavy rightward trudge: front foot plants, body lags, trunk points toward the merge target.
stateAnimations.running-left: Heavy leftward trudge with tusks leading and rear body catching up one beat late.
stateAnimations.waving: Trunk curls into a friendly hook-wave, then uncurls back against the tusk.
stateAnimations.jumping: Not a normal jump: a tiny mammoth hop where knees compress deeply and the body barely lifts.
stateAnimations.failed: Trunk drops into a knot shape, tusks tilt down, and the wool mass slumps.
stateAnimations.waiting: Trunk holds an invisible conflict strand toward the user, asking which side to keep.
stateAnimations.running: Merge-resolution loop: trunk pulls two attached strands together, pauses, then smooths the wool line.
stateAnimations.review: Peers over one tusk, trunk tracing an invisible diff boundary with slow precision.

pets.whyFlagship: CI failure and recovery are the emotional loop of modern engineering; phoenix rebirth is exact.
pets.audienceMoment: A maintainer is watching checks fail, rerun, recover, and finally pass.
pets.characterCore: A compact phoenix whose flame-feathers represent rebuilds and recoveries.
pets.visualLock: Small bird body, flame-feather silhouette, ember accent attached to wings and tail.
animationPrinciples: effects must be attached flame-feathers, not loose particles
failure should become ash posture, recovery should be visible in the body
running is build energy, not foot running

stateAnimations.idle: Flame-feather tips flicker in a contained loop while the bird breathes like a pilot light.
stateAnimations.running-right: Glides right on two compact wing beats, tail flame streaming as attached feather shapes.
stateAnimations.running-left: Glides left with a rebuild-sweep wing cadence and tail flame tucked close.
stateAnimations.waving: Wing opens in a small flame-feather fan, then closes without shedding sparks.
stateAnimations.jumping: Rekindle hop: body dips into ember shape, lifts in a flame-feather flare, then lands brighter.
stateAnimations.failed: Feathers dim and fold into a soft ash-gray hunch before one ember reappears.
stateAnimations.waiting: Holds wings half-open like paused checks, ember tips steady but low.
stateAnimations.running: Build loop: flame-feathers pulse from base to tip as the head scans like a check runner.
stateAnimations.review: Phoenix leans into the result, one wing shielding the eyes while tail ember steadies.

pets.whyFlagship: Patch application is the core agent action; a careful panda gives it charm and precision.
pets.audienceMoment: Codex is applying a small fix, editing files, or preparing a patch for review.
pets.characterCore: A meticulous panda placing tiny patches with careful paws.
pets.visualLock: Round panda silhouette, patch-shaped paw pads or satchel, calm repair energy.
animationPrinciples: patches should be attached to paws or body, not floating
motion should be careful and tactile
avoid generic typing; this pet repairs

stateAnimations.idle: Panda sits compactly and pats one patch pad against the belly in a tiny tidy loop.
stateAnimations.running-right: Soft rightward roll-step with patch satchel tucked close and paws alternating low.
stateAnimations.running-left: Soft leftward roll-step, head leading and round body catching up with a gentle sway.
stateAnimations.waving: One paw lifts, showing a little patch pad as a repair greeting.
stateAnimations.jumping: Round panda bounce: squat, soft lift, belly squash, and controlled landing.
stateAnimations.failed: Patch pad peels halfway in the paw and the panda lowers its head in repair frustration.
stateAnimations.waiting: Holds a patch between both paws toward the user, asking where it should go.
stateAnimations.running: Patch-application loop: paw presses an attached patch, smooths the edge, checks the seam, repeats.
stateAnimations.review: Leans close to inspect the patch edge with one paw bracing the body.

pets.whyFlagship: The repo needs one public, non-Zentrik product-work mascot for PMs and AI-native teams.
pets.audienceMoment: A product lead is turning user intent, roadmap pressure, and implementation detail into a coherent task.
pets.characterCore: A precise planning crane that organizes intent with calm, long-form attention.
pets.visualLock: Elegant crane silhouette, paper-tab wing tips, subtle roadmap chevron feather marks, no readable UI.
animationPrinciples: movement should feel like prioritization and sequencing
avoid literal boards, sticky notes, or text
wing tips act like tabs being sorted

stateAnimations.idle: Crane balances on one leg while two wing-tip tabs settle into alignment.
stateAnimations.running-right: Steps right in deliberate roadmap beats: plant, pause, align tail, continue.
stateAnimations.running-left: Steps left with head low and wing tabs trailing like reordered priorities.
stateAnimations.waving: Wing opens into three small tab-like feathers, then folds back in order.
stateAnimations.jumping: Planning lift: one leg compresses, body rises in a graceful arc, wing tabs tuck at the peak.
stateAnimations.failed: Wing tabs fall out of alignment and the neck lowers as if intent was lost.
stateAnimations.waiting: Extends one tabbed wing toward the user for prioritization input.
stateAnimations.running: Intent-sorting loop: head scans, wing tabs reorder, body gives one decisive nod.
stateAnimations.review: Cranes neck forward into a traceable inspection line while wing tabs lock into final order.
